table.heading: Oseox and Seogard, line by line

rows.criteria: On-page change detection (title, meta, canonical, robots, HTTP, H1)

seo.title: Oseox Alternative: SEO Monitoring That Renders JavaScript | Seogard
seo.description: Oseox crawls without executing JavaScript (their own docs). On a Nuxt, Next or Vue site, SSR/CSR regressions slip through. Seogard diffs raw HTML against the JS render. Free scan, no card.
seo.ogTitle: Oseox Alternative — the monitoring that executes JavaScript
seo.ogDescription: Oseox's crawler doesn't execute JS. Seogard diffs raw HTML and the JavaScript render, as a complement. Free scan.
seo.twitterTitle: Oseox Alternative — the monitoring that executes JavaScript
seo.twitterDescription: Oseox's crawler doesn't execute JS. Seogard diffs raw HTML and JS render. Free scan.

alternativeOseox.badge: A complement to Oseox, not a replacement
alternativeOseox.h1: An Oseox alternative for SEO monitoring
alternativeOseox.tldr: Oseox is a solid SEO regression tool — and they say it themselves: their crawler doesn't execute JavaScript. On a Nuxt, Next, Vue or React stack, it sees the raw HTML, not what the browser actually renders. Seogard compares both, page by page, and alerts you when they diverge. It doesn't replace Oseox: it covers the blind spot.

already.title: Already using Oseox? Good.
already.p1: We're not going to sell you a replacement for a tool that works. Oseox does things Seogard doesn't: rank tracking, keywords, backlinks. Keep it.
already.p2: One question: does your monitoring execute JavaScript? Oseox's doesn't — it's stated plainly in their documentation. On a server-rendered site, the day SSR breaks on 200 pages while the browser keeps rendering the JavaScript, nobody notices. That's the exact scenario Oseox tells you to watch for.
already.p3: Seogard watches it by comparing both renders continuously. Cost to check: 30 seconds, your URL, no credit card.

stack.title: Nuxt, Next, Vue, React SSR: the precise risk
stack.story: Friday, 5pm, the team ships a header redesign. In the browser, everything works. But server-side, rendering broke on 200 product pages — the metas vanished from the raw HTML. Google indexes empty pages. Nobody notices, because the browser executes the JavaScript and shows a correct page.
stack.tech: On these stacks, two versions of your page coexist: the raw HTML the server sends (what Google indexes first, what AI reads) and the DOM after JavaScript hydration (what you see in the browser). Monitoring that doesn't execute JavaScript can only see one side. Seogard measures the gap between both, on every page, on every crawl.

complement.title: Oseox and Seogard: a complement, not a replacement
complement.text: Keep Oseox for rank tracking, keywords and backlinks — it does them well. Add Seogard for the blind spot a no-JS crawl leaves open: SSR/CSR regressions, and visibility to AI engines (llms.txt, GPTBot / ClaudeBot crawlers). The two complement each other.

items.a: No. Their documentation states it explicitly: their crawler does not execute JavaScript. It reads the raw HTML the server returns — enough for most sites, but not enough to detect a gap between the server render and the browser render on a modern JS stack.
No, and that's not the goal. Seogard does no rank tracking, no keywords, no backlinks. It focuses on one angle Oseox doesn't cover: comparing raw HTML and the JavaScript render continuously.
Cloud is 0.01 €/month/monitored page, with a 14-day trial, no credit card. The self-hosted version is free (source available, BSL 1.1 license).

cta.title: Check the gap on your own site
cta.text: Paste your URL: Seogard compares the raw HTML and the JavaScript render and shows you the gap in 30 seconds. Free, no credit card.
cta.selfHosted: Or install the {link}, free and source available.

seo.title: Oseox vs Seogard: SEO Monitoring Compared
seo.description: Oseox: all-in-one SEO suite that crawls without JavaScript. Seogard: SSR/CSR regression detection (raw HTML vs JS render). An honest, sourced side-by-side.
seo.ogTitle: Oseox vs Seogard — the honest comparison
seo.ogDescription: Oseox = broad suite (rank, keywords, backlinks). Seogard = SSR/CSR regression (raw HTML vs JS render). Two different jobs.
seo.twitterTitle: Oseox vs Seogard — the honest comparison
seo.twitterDescription: Oseox = broad suite. Seogard = SSR/CSR regression (raw HTML vs JS render). Factual comparison.

vsSeogard.h1: Oseox vs Seogard: the honest comparison
vsSeogard.tldr: Oseox does rank tracking, keywords and backlinks — Seogard doesn't. Seogard does one thing Oseox admits it doesn't: execute JavaScript to compare the raw HTML against the real render. If your site runs on Nuxt, Next, Vue or React, that's the most expensive blind spot.

positioning.title: Two tools playing different matches
positioning.oseox: Oseox is a broad SEO suite: rank tracking, a keyword database, backlinks, uptime, and on-page change detection that crawls without executing JavaScript.
positioning.seogard: Seogard is a technical-render specialist: it compares the raw HTML (SSR) against the JavaScript render (CSR) on every page, alerts on regressions, and can block a regressive deploy through a CI/CD webhook.

jsDiff.title: The real difference: JavaScript rendering
jsDiff.text: This isn't an opinion, it's written in Oseox's documentation. Their crawler doesn't execute JavaScript. In practice, it can't compare — on the same page at the same moment — the raw HTML and the DOM rendered after JavaScript, the precise gap that reveals an SSR regression on a modern stack.
jsDiff.verbatim: Our crawler does not execute Javascript, so if your prerender fails, you will automatically receive a notification within the hour.
jsDiff.verbatimSource: Source: Oseox documentation (oseox-software.com), collected on 21 July 2026.

pricingNote.title: Price, at equal scope
pricingNote.text: Comparing prices only makes sense at equal scope. Oseox bills a full suite, by URL tier ($99 to $479/month). Seogard bills render monitoring only, usage-based: 0.01 €/month/page, roughly 10 €/month for 1,000 monitored pages. But if you want rank tracking and backlinks, you want Oseox, not us.

choose.title: Which one to choose?
choose.text: Oseox if you want a broad, all-in-one SEO suite. Seogard — or both — if you run a modern JavaScript stack (Nuxt, Next, Vue, React) and SSR/CSR regression is your number-one risk. The only way to settle it: run the scan on your own URL and see whether there's a gap.

cta.title: Check it on your own URL
cta.text: Seogard compares the raw HTML and the JavaScript render and shows you the gap in 30 seconds. Free, no credit card.
